response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack web developer with strong software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD) and some low-latency systems experience in DLang, but the role requires deep hardware-domain expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. While the BS plus 15+ years of experience technically satisfies the educational qualification path, there is no evidence of deep-learning workload optimization, SIMD/MIMD architecture work, or silicon-level performance engineering, making this a fundamental domain mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering experience with a BS in Computer Science, satisfying the baseline experience requirement
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang)
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon design, or hardware architecture experience whatsoever
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or accelerator performance-optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product development, not principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI/ML or GPU workload optimization background
